title: ED overdose documentation-only

red_flags.text: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.
red_flags.rationale: For an overdose occurring in a self-harm context, the exact NICE ED section supports documenting onset, injury or illness severity, emotional state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care and clinician risk context; it does not apply to every accidental overdose.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.12–1.7.20 — emergency-department initial assessment and care

red_flags.text: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.
red_flags.rationale: For intentional overdose or possible self-harm, the exact RCEM section supports a focused mental-health assessment, completed risk discussion, clinician plan and arranged liaison handover only when performed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental-health audit standards for the emergency department

follow_up.text: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.
follow_up.rationale: Psychosocial assessment was reviewed for intentional self-poisoning only; no future-risk prediction was accepted.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

source_limitations: Substance, amount, route, timing, co-ingestants, intent, toxidrome, ECG, laboratory tests, toxicology advice, antidote, treatment, dose, monitoring and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
